meaning: n.基石,门槛,窗台,[地质]岩床
examples: Whitlock was perched on the sill of the room's only window.
A thin, glistening thread of moisture ran along the rough concrete sill.
Once, a bird died on the sill and they spent the entire day in the pantry.
My journey began when I was born an Army brat at Fort Sill in Oklahoma.
The side sill panels and striking bumpers with bar-shaped LED daytime driving lights exude dynamism.
